name: Entity
desc: Base class for entity relative movement and rotation updates. Subclasses S15 (rel move), S16 (look), S17 (move+look).

subclasses.name: S17PacketEntityLookMove
subclasses.desc: Both relative move and rotation update.

notes: S14 is the abstract base — the actual packet IDs are 0x15 (S15), 0x16 (S16), 0x17 (S17). All share the same class hierarchy. The client adds the relative deltas to the entity's current server position to get the new position.

implementation.overview: S14PacketEntity and its subclasses (S15 rel move, S16 look, S17 move+look) provide incremental entity position/rotation updates. Unlike S18 (absolute teleport), S14 applies byte-sized relative deltas (× 32) to the entity's current position. Aqua 13's Backtrack module intercepts S14 (and all subclasses) to track real position on entities for backtrack hit registration — it accumulates the deltas into entity.realPosX/Y/Z for accurate past-position tracking. ESP and KillAura modules also use S14 to update entity positions for ESP rendering and aim prediction.

modules.name: Backtrack Position Tracking (Aqua 13)
modules.purpose: Track entity real positions by accumulating S14 relative deltas. The Backtrack module reads every S14 packet and adds dx/dy/dz to the entity's realPos fields, maintaining an accurate history of where the entity was for backtrack hit registration.
modules.how_it_works: On every S14 packet arrival, the module reads entityId, finds the entity, and adds the relative deltas (func_149062_c/d/e) to the entity's custom realPosX/Y/Z fields. These custom fields track the 'true' position separate from the interpolated render position. When KillAura checks if a target is within range, it uses both the interpolated position and the realPos for hit detection.
modules.vanilla_hook: NetHandlerPlayClient.handleEntityMovement(S14PacketEntity) — applies relative deltas to entity serverPos and interpolates render position over 3 ticks.
modules.anti_cheat_notes: S14 packet tracking for backtrack is passive and undetectable. However, if Backtrack delays S14 packets in its buffer, the entity will appear to move jerkily or 'rewind' when the buffer is flushed, which other players can see.

implementation.general_hooks:
Client: NetHandlerPlayClient.handleEntityMovement(S14PacketEntity) — applies dx/dy/dz × 32 to entity.serverPosX/Y/Z. The render position interpolates from oldPos to serverPos over 50ms.

Aqua 13 Backtrack: intercepts S14, accumulates realPos deltas, and optionally buffers packets for backtrack replay.

Server: EntityTrackerEntry sends the appropriate S14 subclass every tick for each tracked entity.
implementation.client_variations:
Aqua 13: Backtrack tracks S14 realPos (accumulates deltas). Buffers S14 for replay.
Rise 6.0: Backtrack buffers entity movement packets, including S14/S15/S17.
Sigma 4.11: EventPacket handles S14 — used for ESP position sync.
Tenacity 6.0: ESP reads S14 for entity position tracking.
RektSky B5: No specific S14 module.
Jigsaw 0.26: No specific S14 module.
